titleStmt.title: Letter from Margaret Huxley to Thomas Kirkpatrick, March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Margaret Huxley

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Margaret Huxley to Thomas Percy Kirkpatrick relating to the proposed College of Nursing. While she believes that some points need alteration Huxley believes the scheme will help the nursing profession. Additionally, Huxley expresses concern at the nomination of Miss Hill of the Adelaide Hospital and also hopes that the Joint Committee will help nurses gain state registration.In 1915 Margaret Huxley (1856-1940) took over the position as matron of the Dublin University Womenâ€™s Voluntary Aid Detachmentâ€™s Hospital. The VAD was a section of the Red Cross used to train civilian nurses to provide medical assistance during the war. The following year Huxley was tending to casualties of the Easter Rising as she opened her own private nursing home, on Lower Mount Street.From 1910 until 1954 Thomas Kirkpatrick (1869-1954) served as the registrar for the Royal College of Physicians of Ireland. He also served as the general secretary of the Royal Academy of Medicine in Ireland. He took a particular interest in what were then termed venereal diseases; to encourage his patients (many of whom were prostitutes) to attend, he held a clinic for women at a discreet early morning hour to facilitate anonymity.

msIdentifier.repository: Royal College of Physicians of Ireland

correspAction.placeName: Royal College of Physicians, Kildare Street, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Saturday Grand Hotel Malahide Dear D r Kirkpatrick Thanks so much for
letting me see your letter
to Mr Stanley, I return
it as requested, & at the
same time send you
a paper I received from
Miss Haughton yesterday,
it gives one a better
idea as to the proposed
Nursing College that is to
be, with you I begin to
think that they really
mean to work for the
betterment of nurses &
nursing â there remains
some points for alteration. I hear that Miss
Hill of the Adelaide Hospt.
has been nominated on
the first Council, of
all the Dublin Matrons
she probably knows less
than any one of them
the difficulties &needs
that obtain over here.
Would you kindly keep
Miss Haughton's paper
until my return. Thanking you for all
the trouble you are
taking on our behalf
& hoping you will still
aim for State Registration.
We need it badly. Believe me,
Y r sincerely, Mgt. Huxley
